rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOAN PANARELLI
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Determine Persons Entitled To Distribution Of Estate
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The following issues have not been addressed since the initial hearing on this petition (03/20/2023): 1) A certified copy of a spousal property order or an original small estate affidavit with a certified copy of David Lopez' death certificate must be filed to determine Mary Cooley's entitlement to distribution. 2) If Frank Lopez Jr. survived the decedent, a certified copy of letters issued to the personal representative of his estate must be filed, or an original small estate affidavit with a certified copy of his death certificate must be filed to determine Terrance Lopez/Kevin Lopez' entitlement to distribution. 3) A copy of Denise Lopez' birth certificate or other proof of her child-parent relationship with Frank Lopez must be filed. The only information provided by the supplemental papers was her address. A review of the supplemental papers indicate that the following additional issues must also be addressed: 1) No petition has been filed under Probate Code section 200 or 12400 to establish the fact of death of Charles Steven Madrid if he is presumed to be dead. 2) Re the DE-120 form filed on 11/03/2023: i) Notice to Ruth Guffey, Emelia Madrid, Charles Corbera, Maria Rojas, Brandon Sandoval, and Jeanie Sandoval was given on 11/03/2023, less than 15 days before the last hearing on 11/13/2023, and no new notice of this hearing appears to have been given. ii) Notice was not given to the "attorney" for Kemp & Associates. iii) Zachary Madrid's address is different from his address previously verified. 3) Kevin Lopez, identified as a successor of one of the postdeceased heirs, cannot be located to give notice. The Court is to determine whether to take this petition under submission, without determining the shares of Charles Madrid and Michael Madrid Sr. and successors of David Lopez and Frank Lopez Jr. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ALFRED JOSHUA DIXON
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/14/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The statutory fee is miscalculated: the accounting shows $128,047.17 in receipts, not $218,047.17. (2) There are assets on hand as of 7/13/23 that differ from the assets on hand on the date of death, and the gains and losses schedule show different number of shares of most of the funds that were sold, but the accounting does not have an informational schedule. [Probate Code Section 1063(b).] (3) What happened to the automobile? Neither the gains nor losses schedule reports its sale. (4) One of the assets on page 15 of the accounting is truncated. (5) Attorney costs must be itemized to show what the filing fees were for, when they were incurred, and the amount of each, not lump sums. (6) The fourth supplement filed on 12/26/23 actually amended the accounting: the proposed distribution schedule (Exhibit 1) shows slightly different balances in the American Business Bank and Vanguard Brokerage accounts. Mailing notice before the e-filing of the pleading requiring notice is insufficient notice, and 12/26/23 was not at least 15 days before 1/8/24. (7) How will the fractional shares of Doubleline Low Duration Bond CL 1 be distributed? Have they not yet been sold? [NB: LRSF 14.35 I.6.d: "If the assets distributed are not identical to the assets set forth in the Order for Final Distribution, the personal representative must file a fully noticed petition to seek approval of actions taken after the Order for Final Distribution was issued."] (8) Objections filed on10/27/23 have not been addressed. In light of the objections, an appearance will likely be required after all procedural issues are addressed. Deadline is 2/1/24 for delivering the endorsed filed courtesy copies of any responsive filed papers to the assigned examiner. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: DURABLE POWER OF ATTORNEY OF TOM HOUSTON
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION Petition: (I) To Determine Whether Power Of Attorney Is In Effect, And (Ii) To Compell Third Person
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The Court to determine how best to proceed. 1) Camille Houston's Petition for Revocation of Authority of Attorney-in-Fact, etc. a) The prayer includes relief under Probate Code Sections 850 and 859, but this is not disclosed in the caption. [CRC 7.102.] b) If relief is sought under Probate Code Section 850, the incorrect form of Notice of Hearing was served. 2) Notice: a) Camille Houston's consent to electronic service was filed twice on 10/2/23 and 10/3/23. Both were signed by "Ganaa Batsuuri, POA," not by Camille Houston, and neither version had a copy of the durable power of attorney attached to it. b) The consent to electronic service ostensibly signed by or on behalf of Camille Houston and the one signed by Tara Houston have the same email address: ganaa224069@gmail.com. Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE SUSAN MARIKO KOBAYASHI AND CHARLES SUNGWON HAN REVOCABLE TRUST, DATED APRIL 29, 1999
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Modification And Amendment Of Trust
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The Court is to determine whether to take this petition off calendar for lack of diligence, as the following issues (e-mailed to counsel on 06/27/2023 - see also tentative rulings for 08/16/2023 & 10/11/2023 hearings) remain unaddressed: 1) No proposed order has been received or lodged. 2) Is the trust real property community property or either settlor's separate property? Please provide facts in support of the property characterization. 3) Is there any trust asset other than the real property? If so, please provide a brief description, value, and property characterization of each asset. 4) Is the date of birth of each child of the settlors stated in Article 1.4 of the trust true and correct? 5) Have both children of the settlors consented to the proposed elimination of the Bypass Trust? 6) Have Ann Kobayashi and Dale Kobayashi named in Article 7.4 of the trust consented to the proposed designation of the settlors' children as successor trustees of the Bypass Trust? It should be noted that the petitioner's power to designate successor trustees does not apply to the Bypass Trust under Article 7.3. 7) The petition must allege circumstances not known to and anticipated by the deceased settlor re her designation of successor trustees of the Bypass Trust in support of the modification under Probate Code section 15409. 8) Each Article of the trust sought to be modified must be set forth verbatim in the petition and proposed order. 9) Each Article of the trust sought to be deleted in its entirety must be identified as such in the petition and proposed order. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/4/24. The NAME and DATE OF DEATH of the decedent's father was NOT set forth in the supplement filed on 12/8/23. [LRSF 14.18. C. and D.] The last name of the petitioner on form DE-147S is incomplete. Deadline is 2/22/24 for providing the assigned examiner a courtesy copy of the petitioner's verified supplement. [CRC 7.103(c).] (Providing supplemental information should not be "per" issues raised by an examiner; the petitioner should simply supplement her petition.)

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE THOMAS RICHARD JUHL 2008 REVOCABLE TRUST, AS AMENDED
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Instructions Concerning Trust Amendment
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required, although a copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) was not attached to the DE-120 form filed on 12/06/2023. Note: The RA-010 form is NOT the information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6). See the Court's website (divisions - probate court - calendar information - how to make remote appearance for Dept 204). PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.
